evidence.page_title: Dr. Ravinder Pall Saini's Post - LinkedIn
evidence.title: The Jugurtha Tableland in Tunisia is a striking natural mesa that rises about 2,000 feet (600 meters) above the plains, stretching roughly 1,500 meters long and 500 meters wide. Some alternative… | Dr. Ravinder Pall Saini
evidence.description: The Jugurtha Tableland in Tunisia is a striking natural mesa that rises about 2,000 feet (600 meters) above the plains, stretching roughly 1,500 meters long and 500 meters wide. Some alternative theories speculate that such formations may be ancient petrified tree stumps due to their flat tops and vertical structures. While this idea is not scientifically supported, the mesa's impressive scale and structure certainly inspire imaginative geological interpretations.
evidence.text:
The Jugurtha Tableland in Tunisia is a striking natural mesa that rises about 2,000 feet (600 meters) above the plains, stretching roughly 1,500 meters long and 500 meters wide. Some alternative theories speculate that such formations may be ancient petrified tree stumps due to their flat tops and vertical structures. While this idea is not scientifically supported, the mesa's impressive scale and structure certainly inspire imaginative geological interpretations.
It is looking lot like tree fossil
image_captions: View profile for Dr. Ravinder Pall Saini
No alternative text description for this image
